result:
Q2 integrated (FR-019 expanded).

---

**Q3: How onboarding starting numbers seed each program's working state**

Onboarding captures a "working weight or estimated 1RM" per lift, but programs anchor differently — linear programs (5×5/GZCLP) start from a working weight, while 5/3/1 and nSuns run off a **training max** (typically 90% of 1RM). Since all 7 programs are seeded at once, the spec needs one consistent rule converting the entered number into each program's anchor.

**Recommended:** Option A — normalize every entry to an estimated 1RM, then derive each program's anchor from it. This makes one input feed all programs consistently and matches how 5/3/1/nSuns canonically derive their training max.

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| A | Capture/normalize to an estimated 1RM per lift (via Epley if entered as weight×reps); derive each program's anchor from it — TM = 90% of e1RM for 5/3/1 & nSuns; conservative working-weight start for linear programs |
| B | Capture a working weight per lift; use it directly for linear programs and treat it as the training max as-is for 5/3/1/nSuns (no 90% derivation) |
| C | Capture both an e1RM **and** a starting working weight separately; seed each program from whichever it canonically uses |
| Short | Different seeding rule (≤5 words) |

You can reply with the option letter, accept the recommendation ("yes"/"recommended"), or provide your own short answer.
